rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings and assignments.
Use only visible identifiers from the current component scope to avoid unresolved reference errors.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Preserve AADL architecture by avoiding package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
Do not introduce data types, ports, or properties that are not explicitly defined in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and must follow AADL scope rules.
Implementation-level AGREE bindings should reference subcomponents and connections defined in the same scope.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level AGREE bindings in component type scopes where subcomponents are not yet defined.
Do not use package-qualified identifiers unless they are explicitly present in the AADL model and configured dependencies.
